L1.area: Client-side state persistence and HTTP request data handling utilities

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where user-controlled values stored in client-side browser state are passed to PHP's native deserialization mechanism without any validation or object type checking. An attacker can craft a malicious serialized PHP object payload that, when processed by the application during data retrieval, triggers arbitrary code execution through PHP magic methods invoked during the deserialization process.

L3.area: Cookie state management and retrieval within the HTTP data handling subsystem
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where user-controlled cookie values are passed to PHP's native deserialization mechanism without any validation or object type checking when reading stored cookie data. An attacker can craft a malicious serialized PHP object as a cookie value, triggering arbitrary code execution through PHP magic methods like __wakeup() during the deserialization process. The vulnerability is exploitable via the Spoon library's cookie retrieval path used by Fork CMS.

ground_truth.reason: The cookie retrieval method uses PHP's unserialize() on user-controlled cookie values without validation, enabling PHP object injection and arbitrary code execution via magic methods.
